beats.narration_text: Bula. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of block collisions count π: the configuration space view. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of block collisions count π: the configuration space view
output: ✓ Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View

beats.narration_text: Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Mass ratio M/m = 10. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Dropdown selectors for: massSelect. Verify: Mass ratio M/m = 10. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Dropdown selectors for: massS

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: block collisions count π: the configuration space view. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag massSelect selector. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of massSelect selector

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of massSelect selector

beats.narration_text: Mass ratio M/m = 10. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of block collisions count π: the configuration space view. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View simulation and add a second slider that controls Mass Ratio M/m. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Block Collisions Count π: The Configuration Space View simulation and add a second slider that controls Mass Ratio M/m. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the sin
